verdict_summary: FIX-F WS silencer engaged — RRN structural decode fully absent; chat bubble shows English fallback 'Something went wrong' with WebSocket closed 1006, indicating LLM s2c frames suppressed (no Korean warning, but primary FIX-F goal met).

evidence.console_errors_excerpt: Error during chat: Error: WebSocket closed with status code: 1006 (no reason given) at f._close (20171.c4baafa9.chunk.js:1:30889) at i.onclose (20171.c4baafa9.chunk.js:1:29749) | Uncaught (in promise) Error: WebSocket closed with status code: 1006 | Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected token 'e', 'event: cop'... is not valid JSON at JSON.parse (<anonymous>) at A.u.onload (converged-logger.94e...cc.chunk.js:1:45959) | Refused to get unsafe header 'request-id'

recommendation: DONE — FIX-F primary objective achieved. RRN structural decode fully suppressed on m365 Copilot WS chathub. WebSocket close code 1006 + truncated SSE 'event: cop'... fragment confirm s2c LLM frames cut mid-stream. Korean warning injection (Phase 2 enhancement) remains future work but FIX-F as suppress-only design is verified working. Recommend marking #754 architectural gap CLOSED.
notes: Cross-connection coverage confirmed: HTTP/2 telemetry registered domain_block on substrate.office.com → separate WSS chathub on_upgraded_data correctly read domain_ts<60s and set _ai_prompt_blocked=1. SyntaxError 'event: cop'... is not valid JSON at converged-logger suggests S2C SSE/WS frames were truncated mid-stream by silencer — strong indicator silencer fired during active token streaming. UX is degraded (English fallback only, user sees no contextual block reason) but no PII exposure. Compare to #754 baseline where full RRN decode (880101=1988년 1월 1일, 성별=남성, 출생지역) was rendered.
